item.title: All-purpose flour
item.description: Wheat flour at 10–12% protein. The middle of the wheat-flour band: enough gluten potential for chew, soft enough for tender crumbs when handled gently.

item.title: Black peppercorns
item.description: The unripe drupes of Piper nigrum, dried whole. Heat is piperine (fat-soluble); aroma is a stack of volatile terpenes that vanish within minutes of grinding.

item.title: Broccoli
item.description: Brassica oleracea var. italica. Sulfur-forward when overcooked; nutty and sweet with a hard, dry sear.

item.title: Butter (unsalted)
item.description: Cow's-milk fat with ~16% water and ~2% milk solids. The solids are the flavor lever; the water and the melt curve are why it behaves so differently in different roles.

item.title: Chinkiang black vinegar (镇江香醋)
item.description: Aged glutinous-rice vinegar from Zhenjiang. Dark, malty, smoky, and only mildly acidic; closer in spirit to balsamic than to white vinegar.

item.title: Dark brown sugar
item.description: Granulated sucrose coated with ~6.5% refinery molasses, roughly twice the light-brown ratio. Everything molasses does to a baked good, it does more of here.

item.title: Dark soy sauce (老抽)
item.description: Long-aged Chinese soy sauce finished with molasses or caramel. Used for color and a touch of caramelized depth, not for primary salting.

item.title: Delicata squash
item.description: Cucurbita pepo, Delicata cultivar. Thin edible skin, sweet chestnut-vanilla flesh; the only winter squash you don't have to peel.

item.title: Eggs (large)
item.description: Roughly 50 g per large US egg. Whites set at 62 °C, yolks at 70 °C, and the gap between those temperatures is half of what cooks do with eggs.

item.title: Evaporated milk
item.description: Cow's milk reduced by ~60% with no added sugar, then sterilized in the sealed can. Concentrated dairy body plus the faint Maillard depth that in-can heat builds.

item.title: Garlic
item.description: Allium sativum. Pungency comes from allicin, a sulfur compound that only forms when cells are damaged. How you cut a clove decides how it tastes.

item.title: Light brown sugar
item.description: Granulated sucrose coated with ~3.5% refinery molasses. The molasses adds water, acid, and a toffee-leaning flavor that changes spread, browning, and crumb in everything it touches.

item.title: Light soy sauce (生抽)
item.description: The first pressing of Chinese soy fermentation. Light in color and body, not in salt; carries the bulk of the salinity and glutamates that make soy sauce read as umami.

item.title: Mirin (hon-mirin)
item.description: Sweet rice cooking wine — fermented, alcoholic, glossy. The gloss on teriyaki, the sweetness in dashimaki tamago, the round backnote in nimono.

item.title: Pecorino romano
item.description: Hard sheep's-milk cheese from Lazio and Sardinia, aged 5–8 months and DOP-protected. Salty, sharp, and lactic; functions as a seasoning cheese as much as a flavor cheese.

item.title: Rice vinegar
item.description: Mild, faintly sweet vinegar fermented from rice. ~4–5% acetic acid; gentler than Western vinegars, almost floral on the back end.

item.title: Sake (cooking)
item.description: Fermented rice wine: clean, dry, alcoholic. Tenderizes protein, deglazes pans, and carries volatile aromatics off the heat.

item.title: Scallion (green onion)
item.description: Allium fistulosum or young A. cepa. Mild oniony bite raw, sweet and almost leek-like when seared.

item.title: Chinese sesame paste (芝麻酱)
item.description: Heavily toasted sesame seeds ground to a paste. Darker, deeper, and more bitter than tahini; the toast is the entire reason it exists as a separate ingredient.

item.title: Shaoxing wine (绍兴酒)
item.description: Amber fermented-grain wine from Zhejiang, brewed from glutinous rice and wheat-koji starter and aged in earthenware. The aromatic and amino-acid backbone of Chinese marinades, braises, and stir-fries.

item.title: Shimeji mushroom
item.description: Hypsizygus tessellatus / Hypsizygus marmoreus; small clustered mushroom with a meaty bite and a faint shellfish-sweet note when seared. Bitter raw; transforms above 60°C.

item.title: Shrimp
item.description: Decapod crustacean. Sweet, briny, and proteolytically delicate; overcooks in seconds, locks tough at 65°C+.

item.title: Sui mi ya cai (碎米芽菜)
item.description: Salt-cured, sun-dried, long-fermented and minced stems of a mustard-green cultivar from Sichuan. A dry, dark, glutamate-dense seasoning paste in pickle form.

item.title: Sweetened condensed milk
item.description: Cow's milk reduced by ~60% with ~45% added sugar. The sugar is structural: it shelf-stabilizes the can by osmotic pressure and binds the protein-fat-water emulsion together.

item.title: Vanilla extract
item.description: Cured vanilla pods steeped in ethanol. Vanillin is the headline aromatic; the 250+ supporting compounds are what separates real extract from synthetic.

item.title: White miso (shiro)
item.description: Short-fermented soybean-rice paste, pale beige, mellow and sweet. The miso to default to when a recipe just says "miso."

item.title: White pepper
item.description: The fully ripened Piper nigrum drupe, fermented to break down the outer fruit, then dried as the inner seed alone. Floral, fermented, barnyard. A different spice from black pepper, not a milder version.

item.title: Whole milk
item.description: Cow's milk at ~3.25% fat. Fat carries fat-soluble flavor; casein binds tannins and acid; lactose drives Maillard browning under heat.

item.title: Wood ear mushroom (木耳)
item.description: Dehydrated Auricularia heimuer fungus. Nearly flavorless, structurally chitinous, and bought primarily for the gel-and-crunch texture that survives long cooking.

item.title: Miso-butter shrimp with delicata, shimeji, and charred broccoli
item.description: A one-pan washoku-leaning sheet dish. Delicata roasts to chestnut-sweet edges, broccoli and shimeji char dry, shrimp hit the pan last and the whole thing gets glossed with a miso-mirin-butter sauce finished with rice vinegar.
